resposta:
solution: 
```python
from collections import Counter
import heapq

class Solution:
    def topKFrequent(self, words: List[str], k: int) -> List[str]:
        count = Counter(words)
        heap = [(-freq, word) for word, freq in count.items()]
        heapq.heapify(heap)
        return [heapq.heappop(heap)[1] for _ in range(k)]
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log k)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Count frequencies with Counter (O(n)). Build a min-heap of size k keyed by (-frequency, word) so lexicographical order is preserved for ties. Each heap operation is O(log k) and we do n insertions, giving O(n log k). Extracting k items is O(k log k) ≤ O(n log k). Uses O(n) extra space for the Counter and heap.
